name: gql (Python GraphQL Client)

auth.notes: Flexible auth via transport configuration — headers, auth tokens, OAuth

performance.notes: Network-bound; async transport recommended for concurrent queries

agent_readiness.notes: GraphQL client supporting sync, async, and subscriptions. from gql import gql, Client. from gql.transport.aiohttp import AIOHTTPTransport. transport = AIOHTTPTransport(url='https://api.example.com/graphql', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer TOKEN'}). client = Client(transport=transport, fetch_schema_from_transport=True). query = gql('query { user(id: 1) { name email } }'). result = client.execute(query). Async: async with Client(...) as session: result = await session.execute(query). Variables: client.execute(query, variable_values={'id': 1}). Subscriptions: WebsocketsTransport. Batch: RequestsHTTPTransport(use_json=True). Schema validation at runtime. Transports: aiohttp, requests, websockets, phoenix channels.

gotchas: fetch_schema_from_transport=True requires introspection enabled on server
Subscription transport (websockets) requires separate install: gql[websockets]
Error handling differs between transport types — TransportQueryError vs ClientError
Variable injection requires properly typed variable_values dict
best_when: Consuming GraphQL APIs from Python (GitHub API, Shopify, Contentful, etc.)
Async Python clients for GraphQL with aiohttp transport
When schema validation at query time is important
avoid_when: Very simple GraphQL queries (httpx with raw JSON is simpler)
When GraphQL introspection is disabled (validation fails)
Building GraphQL servers (use Graphene or Strawberry)
summary: gql is a Python GraphQL client. Client(transport=AIOHTTPTransport(url, headers)).execute(gql('query ...')). Async, sync, and WebSocket subscription transports. Runtime schema validation. MIT by graphql-python. Best for consuming GraphQL APIs. Requires extra installs per transport (gql[aiohttp], gql[websockets]). introspection needed for schema validation. Type-annotated. Multiple transport backends.
